pattern: Scanning the array once and tracking the smallest index distance to a matching value from the given start position yields the minimum reachable offset.
hints: You only care about positions that actually hold the target value.
For every such position, measure how far it sits from start, and keep the smallest distance.
Loop i over the array; when nums[i] equals target, update the answer with abs(i - start).

edge_inputs.description: single unique match to the right
all equal, nearest is start itself
two matches, must pick the closer one
match at the far left while start is far right

stress.description: large array with guaranteed target presence
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    pos = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    nums[pos] = target
    start = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    return [nums, target, start]
